goal: get practical, actionable advice for an ongoing neighbor dispute
constraints: check lease/HOA rules and local codes for enforceability
be factual, brief, and focused on impact
preserve evidence and maintain a paper trail
check local recording laws and privacy considerations
prefer written communications to document interactions
do not publicize on social media or share unverified information
decisions: start organized record-keeping for potential formal action
classify records by type (incident logs, photos, communications, etc.) to support future steps
consider mediation as initial step where safe and feasible
follow formal complaint process early to create an official record
involve a neutral mediator, building manager, or trusted witness for initial contact
establish an evidence-collection protocol and use the evidence checklist to document incidents
prioritize in-home mitigation steps to protect child health during exposure when escalation is ongoing
draft urgent communication to landlord/management citing child health effects (when appropriate)
adopt dual-channel notice delivery (email plus certified mail or tracked courier) for formal notices to landlords/management
attach supporting photos or logs to notices and keep copies of all sent items and delivery proof
open: timing for escalation to formal complaint
cigarette smoke drifting up from shared staircase into apartment (document incidents; consult management; escalate if unresolved)
dog barking at night next door (document incidents; escalate if unresolved)
determine city-specific quiet hours/noise ordinance resources to consult for guidance on applicable rules
child coughing at night; health concerns; document episodes (dates/times, symptoms) and seek medical advice; attach any medical notes to documentation
identify short-term in-home mitigation steps for protecting child health (air purifiers, room arrangement, sealing gaps)
unauthorized vehicle occupying assigned parking spot; document incidents; notify management; request enforcement/towing per policy

landlord maintenance requests unaddressed; landlord has not responded to follow-ups for months; document communications and consider alternative escalation (mediation, formal complaint, or contacting housing authority) if no timely response
